problem.value: Finance team needs accurate, correctable figures to close books; current reported totals may be wrong or unadjustable at source.
problem.evidence: Request implies totals as displayed are not trustworthy or flexible enough for finance to use directly.

current_process.evidence: No mention of how totals are currently produced (manual calc, invoice engine, mission aggregation).

success_metrics.value: Finance receives an exportable, corrected figure set they can use without manual rework.
success_metrics.evidence: 'export the figures for the finance team' implies usability as the success bar.

actors.value: Unnamed 'users' edit totals; 'finance team' consumes the export.
actors.evidence: Client names 'users' and 'finance team' but not their exact roles (manager? admin? finance itself?).

business_objects.value: 'Reported totals' — unclear if Invoice amounts, Mission billing, Contract totals, or an aggregated report.
business_objects.evidence: 'Reported totals' and 'figures' are generic; product has multiple total-bearing entities (Invoice, Contract, Mission).

business_rules.value: Unknown whether editing a total means overriding a computed figure, editing source line items, or issuing a new versioned document (amendment-style) for an already-issued invoice.
business_rules.evidence: Domain notes: Invoice amount is derived (line items, VAT, numbering); editing an issued financial document is normally a new version, not a patch.

workflow.value: Unclear which lifecycle state the total is in when edited (draft vs issued vs paid) and what re-triggers (re-signature, re-numbering, downstream invoice regeneration).
workflow.evidence: Invoice/Contract lifecycle notes: editing an issued artifact re-opens process, unlike editing a draft.

integrations.value: Export format/destination unknown — flat file, accounting system API, recurring feed vs on-demand.
integrations.evidence: 'Export the figures' without specifying format, cadence, or target system.

permissions.value: Unclear who may edit financial totals (risk of unauthorized changes to legal/financial figures) and who may export/view them.
permissions.evidence: Financial data + French VAT/legal numbering rules make edit/export rights sensitive; permissions default high anyway.

config_vs_custom.evidence: Not stated whether edit/export behavior must vary per client or be a shared standard feature.

constraints.value: French VAT and legal invoice numbering rules may forbid direct editing of an issued invoice total.
constraints.evidence: Context: invoicing follows French VAT and legal numbering rules — editing 'reported totals' could conflict with immutability requirements.

edge_cases.evidence: Not addressed: concurrent edits, retroactive edits on paid invoices, partial/multi-invoice totals.

reporting.value: Export to finance is explicit, but no mention of an audit trail for who changed which total and when.
reporting.evidence: Editing financial totals under VAT/legal-numbering constraints typically requires a change history for compliance — impact raised from default low to high.

risks.evidence: Risk of breaking invoice numbering integrity / legal compliance if edits are unconstrained; not yet discussed.

questions.q: When a user 'edits a total,' are they correcting an issued invoice/contract figure (which should legally create a new versioned document, like an amendment) or only adjusting a draft/pre-issuance figure?
questions.why: High impact × high uncertainty: French legal numbering makes issued-invoice edits a different, heavier build (versioning, re-trigger of downstream automation) than a simple field patch — this changes the entire architecture.

questions.q: Which 'totals' exactly — Invoice amounts, Contract billing totals, Mission/freelancer figures, or an aggregated cross-entity report?
questions.why: High impact × high uncertainty: each source entity has different fields, derivation logic, and lifecycle — the whole scope hinges on this.

questions.q: Who is allowed to edit these totals (finance only, managers, admins?) and is that different from who can trigger the export?
questions.why: High impact × high uncertainty: financial data + regulatory sensitivity means unrestricted edit rights is a compliance risk, not just a UX detail.

questions.q: Do French VAT / legal invoice-numbering rules restrict which totals can be edited once issued, and if so should edits be blocked, require approval, or auto-generate a new document?
questions.why: High impact × high uncertainty: this determines whether 'edit' is even legally permitted as a direct action, and what the fallback mechanism is.

questions.q: Should every edit to a reported total be logged with who/when/old-value-to-new-value for audit purposes, and is the export a one-off manual action or a recurring feed to an accounting system?
questions.why: High impact × high uncertainty: financial edits without an audit trail are a compliance gap; export cadence (manual vs recurring/API) is a different integration build.

summary.objective: Allow authorized users to correct reported financial totals and give the finance team a reliable export of those figures.
summary.scope: Likely covers editing totals on one or more financial entities (Invoice, Contract, or Mission billing), with an export mechanism (file or feed) to the finance team; may require versioning of issued documents instead of direct edits, plus permission controls and an audit trail given French VAT/legal numbering constraints.
assumptions: The 'reported totals' refer to Invoice and/or Contract billing figures rather than an unrelated report.
Users editing totals are likely finance/admin roles, not any employee.
Editing an already-issued invoice total should follow the amendment/versioning pattern rather than an in-place patch.
The export is intended for periodic or on-demand use by an external accounting process.
summary.blind_spot: The request treats 'edit the total' as a simple field change, but if the total belongs to an issued invoice, French legal numbering and VAT rules likely require a new versioned document rather than a direct edit — plus an audit trail of who changed what. Skipping this turns a small UI tweak into a compliance-sensitive workflow change.
